title: Daulatabad Fort
tagline: Daulatabad Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Daulatabad Fort, originally Devagiri Fort, is a historic fortified citadel located in Daulatabad village near Aurangabad, Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Daulatabad Fort, originally Devagiri Fort, is a historic fortified citadel located in Daulatabad village near Aurangabad, Maharashtra, India. It was the capital of the Yadavas (9th century – 14th century CE), for a brief time the capital of the Delhi Sultanate (1327–1334), and later a secondary capital of the Ahmadnagar Sultanate (1499–1636). Around the 6th century CE, Devagiri emerged as an important uplands town near present-day Aurangabad, along caravan routes going towards western and southern India. The historical triangular fortress in the city was initially built around 1187 by the firs

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chand Minar
topPlaces.description: The Chand Minar or the Tower of the Moon is a medieval tower in Daulatabad, India. The tower is located in the state of Maharashtra near the Daulatabad fort complex.

topPlaces.name: Ganj Rawan Ganj Baksh
topPlaces.description: Ganj Rawan Ganj Baksh belonged to the Suhrawardiyya order of Sufi saints, which was founded by Shahab ud din at Baghdad in Hijri 602. He was the earliest of Sufi saint of the Deccan.

topPlaces.name: Zainuddin Shirazi
topPlaces.description: Hazrat Khwaja Syed Shah Maqdoom Zain-ud-din Davud ibn Hussain Shirazi is a Sufi saint of the Deccan, belonging to the Chishti Order.

topPlaces.name: Burhanuddin Gharib
topPlaces.description: Burhanuddin Gharib (d. 1340) was an Indian Sufi of the Chishti Order.

topPlaces.name: Bani Begum Garden
topPlaces.description: Bani Begum Garden is a Mughal garden located in Khuldabad, in the Indian state of Maharashtra. The garden contains the tomb of Jahan Bano Begum, who was the consort of the Mughal prince Bidar Bakht.

topPlaces.name: Shuli Bhanjan
topPlaces.description: Shuli Bhanjan is a hill in the Indian state of Maharashtra located 30 kilometers from Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar (previously known as Aurangabad) on the way to UNESCO Ellora Caves which is just seven kilometers away. This place is situated behind Daulatabad fort as seen from Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar in district of Aurangabad and is accessible by road.

topPlaces.name: Khuldabad
topPlaces.description: Khuldabad, also called Rauza, meaning Garden of paradise, is a city (municipal council) and a Taluka of Aurangabad district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is known as the Valley of Saints, or the Abode of Eternity, because in the 14th century, several Sufi saints chose to reside here.

topPlaces.name: Zar Zari Zar Baksh
topPlaces.description: Zar Zari Zar Baksh (died 15 August 1309), or Shah Muntajab ud din, was one of the earliest Sufis of the Chishti Order, the most dominant of all the Sufi orders in the Indian subcontinent. He was sent to the Deccan by Nizamuddin Auliya of Delhi in the beginning of the 14th century.

items.note: The Chand Minar or the Tower of the Moon is a medieval tower in Daulatabad, India. The tower is located in the state of Maharashtra near the Daulatabad fort complex.
Ganj Rawan Ganj Baksh belonged to the Suhrawardiyya order of Sufi saints, which was founded by Shahab ud din at Baghdad in Hijri 602. He was the earliest of Sufi saint of the Deccan.
Hazrat Khwaja Syed Shah Maqdoom Zain-ud-din Davud ibn Hussain Shirazi is a Sufi saint of the Deccan, belonging to the Chishti Order.
Burhanuddin Gharib (d. 1340) was an Indian Sufi of the Chishti Order.
Bani Begum Garden is a Mughal garden located in Khuldabad, in the Indian state of Maharashtra. The garden contains the tomb of Jahan Bano Begum, who was the consort of the Mughal prince Bidar Bakht.

Shuli Bhanjan is a hill in the Indian state of Maharashtra located 30 kilometers from Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar (previously known as Aurangabad) on the way to UNESCO Ellora Caves which is just seven kilometers away. This place is situated behind Daulatabad fort as seen from Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar in district of Aurangabad and is accessible by road.

Khuldabad, also called Rauza, meaning Garden of paradise, is a city (municipal council) and a Taluka of Aurangabad district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is known as the Valley of Saints, or the Abode of Eternity, because in the 14th century, several Sufi saints chose to reside here.

Zar Zari Zar Baksh (died 15 August 1309), or Shah Muntajab ud din, was one of the earliest Sufis of the Chishti Order, the most dominant of all the Sufi orders in the Indian subcontinent. He was sent to the Deccan by Nizamuddin Auliya of Delhi in the beginning of the 14th century.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Daulatabad Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Daulatabad Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Daulatabad Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Aurangabad Airport (21 km) and the nearest railway station is Aurangabad Railway Station (13 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Daulatabad Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Daulatabad Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Daulatabad Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Daulatabad Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Daulatabad Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chand Minar, Ganj Rawan Ganj Baksh, Zainuddin Shirazi, Burhanuddin Gharib, Bani Begum Garden.
